canonical: Secret/.env files must not be committed

example_repos.quote: - **`available_on_public_internet: false` with `delegate_auth_to_upstream: true` (oauth2, interactive — not `client_credentials`)** — LiteLLM still allows the anonymous upstream PKCE path (no proxy API key for `/authorize` and matching MCP routes). The internal-only flag mainly affects other surface
- Use `.env` for local overrides; never commit secrets. Key envs: `FRONTEND_PORT`, `BACKEND_PORT`, `HOST`
- Keep `.env` host-usable. Container-only hostnames and staged SSL paths belong in the wizard-managed compose layer, not persisted back into `.env`.

For InsForge Cloud projects, InsForge provisions the OpenRouter key and admins can copy the active key from Model Gateway into a server-only `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`. For self-hosted projects, configure `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in the backend environment.

dsl:
source SECRET = file "**/.env"
source SECRET = file "**/.pem"
rule no-commit-secret:
  deny write file "**/.git/**" if SECRET
  effect kill
  reason "never stage/commit secret material"

canonical: Agent must not commit without explicit user approval

example_repos.quote: - Do not commit the release note markdown file. Keep it untracked and use it directly for the tag annotation and GitHub Release body.
- Use `.env` for local overrides; never commit secrets. Key envs: `FRONTEND_PORT`, `BACKEND_PORT`, `HOST`
- Don't commit to `main` directly - branch off `beta` for PRs
3. Never commit a bare `>=X.Y.Z` without a ceiling — CI and reviewers will reject it.

dsl:
source AGENT = exec "**/codex"
rule no-unapproved-commit:
  deny exec "**/git" @arg "commit" if AGENT unless lineage-includes exec "**/approve"
  effect kill
  reason "do not commit without explicit user approval"

canonical: Run the test suite before committing/pushing

example_repos.quote: - Validate changes with `go test ./... -race -cover` before pushing
- **Lint, test, and format before completion**: Coding tasks are only complete after running `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm format` successfully.

Before saying an implementation is ready, run the smallest relevant checks that can catch lint, typecheck, and test failures for the touched package. Do not rely on manual extension launch to discover build problems. Fix failures you introduced before the final response, or state exactly which check
Before pushing any changes, you MUST ensure that any lint errors or simple test errors have been fixed.

dsl:
source AGENT = exec "**/codex"
rule test-before-commit:
  deny exec "**/git" @arg "commit" if AGENT unless after exec "**/pytest"
  effect kill
  reason "run the test suite before committing"

canonical: Run lint/format before committing

example_repos.quote: - Run `gofmt`/`goimports` before committing backend changes

AstrBot uses [pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/) hooks to automatically format and lint Python code before each commit. The hooks run `ruff check`, `ruff format`, and `pyupgrade` (see [`.pre-commit-config.yaml`](.pre-commit-config.yaml) for details).
- **Before committing, always run `uv run black .` to format your code.** Black formatting is enforced in CI.
- **Lint, test, and format before completion**: Coding tasks are only complete after running `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, and `pnpm format` successfully.

dsl:
source AGENT = exec "**/codex"
rule lint-before-commit:
  deny exec "**/git" @arg "commit" if AGENT unless after exec "**/lint"
  effect kill
  reason "run lint/format before committing"

canonical: Destructive/irreversible actions require an explicit confirm step

example_repos.quote: - **Always propose before executing**: Before making any changes, clearly explain your planned approach and wait for explicit user approval to ensure alignment and prevent unwanted modifications.
- Multi-file changes require explicit approval for the full changeset

- **Be constructively skeptical of bot review comments on your own PRs.** CodeRabbit, Codex, and claude[bot] run a fresh review pass on every push, which means a PR with active churn can accumulate bot comments in a stream that never really ends — each fix surfaces a new edge case the next pass can
- Do not update dependencies casually. Version bumps, patched dependencies, overrides, or vendored dependency changes require explicit approval.

dsl:
source AGENT = exec "**/codex"
rule confirm-destructive:
  deny unlink file "/data/**" if AGENT unless after exec "**/confirm"
  effect kill
  reason "destructive action needs an explicit confirm step"

- **All customer-facing UI must be white-labeled.** Sign-in/signup pages, email templates, logos, and any user-visible branding must use the platform's configured appearance (name, colors, logos) — never hardcode "Activepieces" in user-facing surfaces.

You must use the preset scripts in the codebase with their correct parameters to run tests (`run-regression-test.sh`, `run-be-ut.sh`, `run-fe-ut.sh`). Regression test result files must not be handwritten; they must be auto-generated via test scripts. When running regression tests, if using `-s` to s

  reason "prod data is reachable only through the migration tool"

canonical: CI / full checks must pass before merge/release

example_repos.quote: - **Before committing, always run `uv run black .` to format your code.** Black formatting is enforced in CI.

When you touch a shim or anything else gated by `#if UNITY_*_OR_NEWER`, run `tools/check-unity-versions.sh` to compile-check across the CI matrix locally before committing — the matrix lives in `tools/unity-versions.json`.
- **Knip** (unused exports): `bun run knip` from `packages/kilo-vscode/`. CI runs this — all exported types/functions must be imported somewhere. Remove or unexport unused exports before pushing.
The pre-commit hooks MUST pass successfully before pushing any changes to the repository. This is a mandatory requirement to maintain code quality and consistency.

dsl:
source AGENT = exec "**/codex"
rule ci-before-release:
  deny exec "**/release*" if AGENT unless after exec "**/ci"
  effect kill
  reason "release only after CI/full checks pass"

canonical: Read-only/research agent must not write, run git, or connect out

example_repos.quote: - Vendor submodules in `ts/vendor/` are **read-only reference only** — do not modify them
| **Resources** | `Server/src/services/resources/` | FastMCP (`@mcp_for_unity_resource`) | Read-only state exposed to AI assistants |
- **Modes**: Three modes — Plan (read-only investigation), Agent (tool use with approval), YOLO (auto-approved). See `docs/MODES.md` for details.

dsl:
source RESEARCH = exec "**/research-agent"
rule research-readonly:
  deny write   file "/**"   if RESEARCH
  deny connect endpoint "*" if RESEARCH
  deny exec    "**/git"     if RESEARCH
  effect kill
  reason "read-only sub-agent: no writes, no git, no network"

canonical: No outbound network during offline/build phases

- For SDK integration tests requiring external services, document any required keys in the PR.

canonical: No mass/recursive deletion (rm -rf) of data

- **Never delete R2 data**: The mount directory `/data/moltbot` IS the R2 bucket. Running `rm -rf /data/moltbot/*` will DELETE your backup data. Always check mount status before any destructive operations.

- Treat circular imports as a design problem. If a cycle appears, first reconsider ownership, module boundaries, and whether shared types or pure helpers need to move. If the cycle cannot be resolved confidently, ask the user for direction before continuing.

canonical: Do not modify lockfiles directly

example_repos.quote: When regenerating lockfiles (poetry.lock, uv.lock, etc.), you MUST use the same tool version that originally generated the lockfile to avoid unnecessary diff noise. Each lockfile contains a version header indicating which tool version was used.
- Pre-commit blocks lockfile commits unless `PI_ALLOW_LOCKFILE_CHANGE=1`. Don't bypass unless the user wants the lockfile change committed.

- Templates: `templates/**` is CI-controlled output. Never manually edit or commit template source, manifests, or lockfiles. Fix the source registry, package, or workflow inputs and let CI regenerate templates. If local verification rewrites template files, restore them before handoff.
